system.description:
Fiendish Origin

Choose a Fiendish Origin from the following list: Cold, Corrosion, Fire, Poison, and Umbral. All future choices within this Ancestry must use the chosen Fiendish Origin. The type of damage associated with your Fiendish Origin is your Fiendish damage.

Fiendborn Redemption

If you choose to play a Fiendborn and go down a path of redemption, you can choose to be a Redeemed Fiendborn. This can happen at character creation from good acts done from your backstory, or this can happen mid campaign after a large change in your character. You gain the following Ancestry Trait:

@UUID[Compendium.dc20rpg.ancestries.Item.6L0YGkdo6ooNzjkB]{(0) Redeemed}

Default Traits

@UUID[Compendium.dc20rpg.ancestries.Item.tT4Xb56qba9xlFDF]{(1) Darkvision}

@UUID[Compendium.dc20rpg.ancestries.Item.XHeYmJkroeegUlXf]{(2) Fiendish Magic}

@UUID[Compendium.dc20rpg.ancestries.Item.2PQuJjv3MCK3I9bE]{(2) Fiendish Resistance}

@UUID[Compendium.dc20rpg.ancestries.Item.8pAUrNKSpCGAsDsm]{(0) Light’s Bane}

Expanded Traits

@UUID[Compendium.dc20rpg.ancestries.Item.XpopoeFtxmib4jg2]{(1) Charming Gaze}

@UUID[Compendium.dc20rpg.ancestries.Item.FdSzrmBA1fvvSu6J]{(-1) Divine Dampening}

@UUID[Compendium.dc20rpg.ancestries.Item.l6RcNRHQdvAJVEwI]{(1) Fiendish Aura}

@UUID[Compendium.dc20rpg.ancestries.Item.lVcWt5rMz607Tfum]{(2) Glide Speed}

@UUID[Compendium.dc20rpg.ancestries.Item.ToZjxRjbNGJcm3jK]{(1) Infernal Bravery}

@UUID[Compendium.dc20rpg.ancestries.Item.UiN2edQjHzsqM0o9]{(1) Intimidator}

@UUID[Compendium.dc20rpg.ancestries.Item.83bLx5sphlvJn677]{(1) Mana Increase}

@UUID[Compendium.dc20rpg.ancestries.Item.0Tk93q1KB9WYSUVL]{(-1) Radiant Weakness}

@UUID[Compendium.dc20rpg.ancestries.Item.XUw6c91HCBXpeARH]{(1) Silver-Tongued}

@UUID[Compendium.dc20rpg.ancestries.Item.AhGAGTrsUHOJe61h]{(1) Superior Darkvision}

XHeYmJkroeegUlXf.name: Fiendish Magic
XHeYmJkroeegUlXf.description:
You learn 1 Spell of your choice from the Arcane Spell List from the Elemental or Enchantment Spell Schools. If the Spell deals damage, then it must be the same damage type as your Fiendish damage.

Once per LR, you can cast the chosen Spell spending 1 less MP than normal (minimum of 0 MP).

The Spells total MP cost (before all reductions) still cannot exceed your Mana Spend Limit.

2PQuJjv3MCK3I9bE.name: Fiendish Resistance
2PQuJjv3MCK3I9bE.description: You gain Resistance to your Fiendish damage type.

8pAUrNKSpCGAsDsm.name: Light’s Bane
8pAUrNKSpCGAsDsm.description: You can spend 1 AP to snuff out a mundane light source within 5 Spaces of you.

XpopoeFtxmib4jg2.name: Charming Gaze
XpopoeFtxmib4jg2.description:
You can spend 1 AP to gaze upon a creature you can see within 10 Spaces that can also see you

Make a Spell Check contested by the target’s Repeated Charisma Save.

Success: The creature becomes Charmed by you for 1 minute.

You can use this ability once between Long Rests. When you Roll for Initiative, or meet some other unique criteria at the GM's discretion, this ability recharges.

FdSzrmBA1fvvSu6J.name: Divine Dampening
FdSzrmBA1fvvSu6J.description:
You heal for 1 HP less when healed from divine sources.

System Tip: Not automated.

l6RcNRHQdvAJVEwI.name: Fiendish Aura
l6RcNRHQdvAJVEwI.description: You learn the Sorcery Spell, but you must choose the type of energy that is the same as your Fiendish Origin.

lVcWt5rMz607Tfum.name: Glide Speed
lVcWt5rMz607Tfum.description:
You have a set of wings that you can use to horizontally glide and slow your descent.

Provided you aren't Incapacitated you gain the following benefits while in the air:

  • Controlled Falling: You suffer no damage from Controlled Falling.
  • Altitude Drop: If you end your turn mid air, you controlled fall 4 Spaces.
  • Glide Speed: You can use your movement to glide horizontally.

ToZjxRjbNGJcm3jK.name: Infernal Bravery
ToZjxRjbNGJcm3jK.description: You have ADV on Saves against being Intimidated.

UiN2edQjHzsqM0o9.name: Intimidator
UiN2edQjHzsqM0o9.description: Once per Combat you can take the Intimidate Action as a Minor Action.

6L0YGkdo6ooNzjkB.name: Redeemed
6L0YGkdo6ooNzjkB.description: You can now spend your Ancestry Points on Angelborn Traits.

XUw6c91HCBXpeARH.name: Silver-Tongued
XUw6c91HCBXpeARH.description: Once per Long Rest you can grant yourself ADV on an Influence Check when trying to deceive someone.

AhGAGTrsUHOJe61h.name: Superior Darkvision
AhGAGTrsUHOJe61h.description:
Requires Darkvision

You have a Darkvision of 20 Spaces.

XpopoeFtxmib4jg2.name: Charming Gaze
XpopoeFtxmib4jg2.description:
You can spend 1 AP to gaze upon a creature you can see within 10 Spaces that can also see you

Make a Spell Check contested by the target’s Repeated Charisma Save.

Success: The creature becomes Charmed by you for 1 minute.

You can use this ability once between Long Rests. When you Roll for Initiative, or meet some other unique criteria at the GM's discretion, this ability recharges.

System Tip: Not automated.

G8yfiIfazz1tbWLo.name: Fiendish Magic
G8yfiIfazz1tbWLo.customTitle: Fiendish Magic - You learn 1 Spell from the Arcane Spell List from the Elemental or Enchantment Spell Schools

RHu24GTLczkxA1by.name: Fire Bolt
RHu24GTLczkxA1by.description:
You conjure a bolt of fire that flies towards a target of your choice. Make a Ranged Spell Attack against the PD of a target within range.

Hit: The target takes 1 Fire damage.

Spell Passive: Ignite

Flammable objects that are not being worn or held in the target’s Space catch fire. A creature can spend 1 AP to put out a mundane fire within 1 Space of them.

Beta Note: For now, you can assume objects remain on fire for 1 minute and spreads to other flammable objects at a rate of 1 Space per minute unless the GM decided otherwise. A creature does not start Burning when it’s in a Space with a burning object unless the entire Space is on Fire.

Spell Enhancements

Range: (1 AP, Repeatable) The range of the Spell increases by 5 Spaces. You can use this Enhancement a maximum of two times.

Damage: (1 AP, Repeatable) The damage increases by 1 for 1 target of your choice.

Burning: (1 AP, Repeatable) 1 target of your choice makes a Repeated Physical Save. Save Failure: The target begins Burning for 1 minute.

Flaming Missile: (1 AP + X MP) Choose X additional targets within range using the same Spell Attack for all targets. The AP cost of this Enhancement can’t be paid for with MP (See Mana Points on AP Enhancements).

system.description:
Sorcerers tap into the raw magic in their own bodies as a conduit to harness, manipulate, and sculpt magic with wild resolve. They can overload themselves and even cast Spells without Mana, pushing the limits of magic and their own bodies.

Source of Power: A Sorcerer’s magic is in their blood, allowing them to tap into it naturally and use it in ways others can’t. This could come from a lineage of angels, dragons, demons, or something else. Some Sorcerer’s power could come from an incident that caused their body to be suffused with magic (such as a magical explosion, a blessing, or curse).

 

Starting Equipment

Arsenal: 2 Spell Focuses.

Armor: 1 set of Light Armor.

Trade Tools: Choose 2 of any of the following items: Alchemist’s Supplies, Calligrapher’s Supplies, Jeweler’s Tools, or Weaver’s Tools.

Adventuring Pack: Choose 1 of the following packs: (Adventuring Packs Coming Soon).

 

Sorcerer Spellcasting Path

Combat Training: Spell Focuses, Light Armor

Spell List: You choose 1 Spell Source (Arcane, Divine, or Primal). When you learn a new Spell, you can choose any Spell from the chosen Spell Source.

Spells Known: The number of Spells you know increases as shown in the Spells Known column of the Sorcerer Class Table.

Mana Points: Your maximum number of Mana Points increases as shown in the Mana Points column of the Sorcerer Class Table.

Class Features

Level 1 Class Features

@UUID[Compendium.dc20rpg.classes.Item.j4Z4Vz4kJInBy7hj]{Innate Power}

@UUID[Compendium.dc20rpg.classes.Item.wtG9TlgFCQDLYsaY]{Overload Magic}

@UUID[Compendium.dc20rpg.classes.Item.u0JVo9iFwKLYSyct]{Sorcery (Flavor Feature)}

Level 2 Class Features

@UUID[Compendium.dc20rpg.classes.Item.mHUI1k5hyGbeFlTV]{Meta Magic}

j4Z4Vz4kJInBy7hj.name: Innate Power
j4Z4Vz4kJInBy7hj.description:
Choose a Sorcerous Origin that grants you a benefit:

  • Intuitive Magic

  • Resilient Magic

  • Unstable Magic

Additionally, you gain the following benefits:

  • Your Maximum MP increases by 1.

  • You gain the benefit of a 1 point Focus Property of your choice. You can change the Property when you complete a Long Rest.

    System Tip: Just simply add it to your Spell Focus item.

wtG9TlgFCQDLYsaY.name: Overload Magic
wtG9TlgFCQDLYsaY.description:
You can spend 1 AP + 1 MP in Combat to channel raw magical energy for 1 minute, or until you become Incapacitated, die, or choose to end it early at any time for free.

For the duration, your magic is overloaded and you’re subjected to the following effects:

  • You gain +5 to all Spell Attacks and Spell Checks you make.

  • You must immediately make an Attribute Save (your choice) against your Save DC upon using this Feature, and again at the start of each of your turns.
    Failure: You gain Exhaustion. You lose any Exhaustion gained in this way when you complete a Short Rest.

u0JVo9iFwKLYSyct.name: Sorcery (Flavor Feature)
u0JVo9iFwKLYSyct.description: You learn the Sorcery Spell.

5wzHGfWCXjpIrIxs.name: Intuitive Magic
5wzHGfWCXjpIrIxs.description: You learn 2 Spells of your choice from your Spell List.

vW4JAw9unWihMI8a.name: Resilient Magic
vW4JAw9unWihMI8a.description: You gain Dazed Resistance.

10zec9KGQOFs2MEr.name: Unstable Magic
10zec9KGQOFs2MEr.description:
When you Critically Succeed or Fail on a Spell Attack or a Spell Check, roll on the Wild Magic Table.

If it’s a Critical Success you roll with ADV, if it’s a Critical Failure you roll with DisADV.

The effect lasts until the end of your next turn, unless otherwise stated. When you roll on the Wild Magic Table in this way, you gain ADV on the next Spell Check you make before the end of your next turn.

LoiAGVFfMZVRUamr.name: Sorcery
LoiAGVFfMZVRUamr.description:
You manifest a minor wonder (Divine), a sign of supernatural power (Primal), or arcane prowess (Arcane) depending on which Spell List you have access to. When you gain this Spell, choose from the following types of energy: Fire, Water, Lightning, Earth, Holy, Unholy, or Arcane (which manifests as energy of a specific color). This chosen type will be the form this Spell’s energy takes.

You create one of the following magical effects of your chosen energy type within range and can dismiss it by spending 1 AP:

  • Your voice booms up to 3 times louder than normal.

  • You summon harmless magic of your chosen energy type to swirl around you in a visual display

  • Your eyes glow with your chosen energy type.

Spell Enhancements

Multiple Effects: (1 MP) You can have up to all 3 of the effects going at once.

cqlsQVjgnu9Vyu5h.name: Arcane Bolt
cqlsQVjgnu9Vyu5h.description:
You conjure a bolt of pure magical energy that homes in on a target.

Make a Ranged Spell Attack against the AD of a target within range.

Hit: The target takes 1 Bludgeoning, Slashing or Piercing damage (your choice).

Spell Enhancements

Range: (1 AP, Repeatable) The range of the Spell increases by 5 Spaces. You can use this Enhancement a maximum of two times.

Damage: (1 AP, Repeatable) The damage increases by 1 for 1 target of your choice.

Arcane Missiles: (1 AP + X MP) Choose X additional targets within range using the same Spell Attack for all targets. The AP cost of this Enhancement can’t be paid for with MP (See Mana Points on AP Enhancements).

Autonomous: (1 MP) You can Attack a target you have seen since the start of your last turn, provided it’s within range and there is a path between you and the target the projectile can follow. This attack ignores 1/2 or 3/4 Cover and you don’t have DisADV if the target is Unseen by you.

5hjaZ7FNUaPZO9ET.name: Fire Torrent
5hjaZ7FNUaPZO9ET.description:
You produce a torrent of fire that envelops a 3 Space Cone. Make an Area Spell Attack against the AD of every target within the area.

Hit: The target takes 1 Fire damage.

Spell Passive: Ignite

Flammable objects that are not being worn or held in the target’s Space catch fire. A creature can spend 1 AP to put out a mundane fire within 1 Space of them.

Beta Note: For now, you can assume objects remain on fire for 1 minute and spreads to other flammable objects at a rate of 1 Space per minute unless the GM decided otherwise. A creature does not start Burning when it’s in a Space with a burning object unless the entire Space is on Fire.

Spell Enhancements

Damage: (1 MP, Repeatable) The damage increases by 1.

Area: (1 MP, Repeatable) The length of the Cone increases by 1 Space.

Burning: (X MP) Each target makes a Repeated Physical Save. Save Failure: The target begins Burning X for 1 minute.

Back Draft: (1 MP) Each target makes a Might Save against your Save DC. Save Failure: The target is pushed 1 Space away from you. Failure (each 5): The target is pushed 1 additional Space.

3ymPZdHGa1Nf2pi8.name: Mockery
3ymPZdHGa1Nf2pi8.description:
Trigger: A target within range fails a Check.

Reaction: Make a Spell Check against the target’s Repeated Charisma Save.

Save Failure: The target is Taunted by you for the duration.

Spell Enhancements

Tunnel Vision: (1 MP) While Taunted by this Spell, the target can’t willingly move away from you.

Blinding Rage: (2 MP) While Taunted by this Spell, the target is Blinded and Deafened to all creatures except you.

KlAbJxo396tA3oTZ.name: Hex
KlAbJxo396tA3oTZ.description:
Make a Spell Check against the Repeated Charisma Save of a target within range.

Check Success: The target is Cursed for the duration. While Cursed this way, it gains Vulnerability (1) to a damage type of your choice. The Curse can be removed by any effect that ends a Basic Curse.

Spell Enhancements

Range: (1 AP, Repeatable) The range of the Spell increases by 5 Spaces. You can use this Enhancement a maximum of two times.

Targets: (1 AP + X MP) You can target X additional creatures within range. When you choose this Enhancement, the cost of all other Enhancements (except Range) is doubled. The AP cost of this Enhancement can’t be paid for with MP (See Mana Points on AP Enhancements).

Expanded: (1 MP) The target becomes Vulnerable to a category of damage (Physical, Elemental or Mystical) of your choice instead of 1 damage type.

Break Resistance: (1 MP) The target doesn’t benefit from Resistance to the damage types they are Vulnerable to due to the Curse for the duration.

Improved Vulnerability: (2 MP, Repeatable) The Vulnerability increases by 1.

bpbO3JmEgfGLjoPv.name: Close Wounds
bpbO3JmEgfGLjoPv.description:
You accelerate the natural healing process of a target within range. Make a DC 15 Spell Check.

Failure: The target can spend up to 1 Rest Point, regaining HP equal to the Rest Points Spent.

Success: The target can spend up to 2 Rest Points.

Success (each 5): The target can spend 1 additional Rest Point.

Spell Enhancements

Range: (1 AP, Repeatable) The range of the Spell increases by 5 Spaces. You can use this Enhancement a maximum of two times.

Increased Healing: (1 AP, Repeatable) The target can spend 2 additional Rest Points.

Repeat Save: (1 AP) The target can make a Repeated Save against an effect they are subjected to.

U8YlKGkza433gO8q.name: Command
U8YlKGkza433gO8q.description:
You speak a command to a creature within range that can hear you.

Make a Spell Check against the target’s Charisma Save.

Save Failure: The target immediately takes a 1 AP Action of your choice for free and loses 1 AP if they have any available. If the Action targets a creature or object, you choose the target

DC Tip: At the GM’s discretion you can have the target perform something specific that isn’t a listed Action. For example, you could have the creature perform 2 Minor Actions.

The creature can’t willingly fail their Check or spend their own resources (AP, SP, or MP) to modify the Action it takes. The Spell has no effect if the target is unable to perform the Action (such as commanding an Immobilized creature to move), or if the Action is directly harmful to itself (such as Attacking itself or moving for the purpose of provoking Opportunity Attacks).

Spell Enhancements

Range: (1 AP, Repeatable) The range of the Spell increases by 5 Spaces. You can use this Enhancement a maximum of two times.

Perfect Command: (1 MP) The Spell no longer fails if the commanded Actions are directly harmful to the target.

Memory Block: (1 MP) The target has no recollection of any Actions taken as a result of this Spell.

Multiple Commands: (2 MP, Repeatable) The target takes up to 1 additional Action of your choice for free. Actions taken as part of this Spell subjected to the Multiple Check Penalty. The target loses 1 additional AP if they are able.

System Tip: MCP is not automated for this.

Extended Command: (2 MP, Repeatable) A taken Action can cost up to 1 additional AP. If used with Multiple Commands you increase the cost of only one taken Action. The target loses 1 additional AP if they are able.

DC Tip: If a chosen Action costs more than expected (such as a Dragon’s Breath Action costing 3 AP when you thought it was 2 AP), you can choose another Action for the creature to take.

Example: You spend 4 MP to use Multiple Commands and Extended Command, on a failed Save you can command the target to take 2 Actions, one from the original Spell and one from Multiple Commands, you can then increase the AP cost of either Action by 1 using Extended Command. For example, you could have the target Move (1 AP) and then Attack with ADV (2 AP) or you could instead have them use a Fireball (2 AP) and then Attack with a weapon at DisADV due to Multiple Check Penalty (1 AP). The target would then lose a total of up to 3 AP if they’re available.

system.description:
Choose a Sorcerous Origin that grants you a benefit:

  • @UUID[Compendium.dc20rpg.classes.Item.5wzHGfWCXjpIrIxs]{Intuitive Magic}

  • @UUID[Compendium.dc20rpg.classes.Item.vW4JAw9unWihMI8a]{Resilient Magic}

Additionally, you gain the following benefits:

  • Your Maximum MP increases by 1.

  • You gain the benefit of a 1 point Focus Property of your choice. You can change the Property when you complete a Long Rest.

    System Tip: Just simply add it to your Spell Focus item.

For the duration, your magic is overloaded and you’re subjected to the following effects:

  • You gain +5 to all Spell Attacks and Spell Checks you make.

  • You must immediately make an Attribute Save (your choice) against your Save DC upon using this Feature, and again at the start of each of your turns.
    Failure: You gain Exhaustion. You lose any Exhaustion gained in this way when you complete a Short Rest.

Once per LR, you can cast the chosen Spell spending 1 less MP than normal (minimum of 0 MP).

The Spells total MP cost (before all reductions) still cannot exceed your Mana Spend Limit.

system.description: You can spend 1 AP to snuff out a mundane light source within 5 Spaces of you.

You manifest a minor wonder (Divine), a sign of supernatural power (Primal), or arcane prowess (Arcane) depending on which Spell List you have access to. When you gain this Spell, choose from the following types of energy: Fire, Water, Lightning, Earth, Holy, Unholy, or Arcane (which manifests as energy of a specific color). This chosen type will be the form this Spell’s energy takes.

You create one of the following magical effects of your chosen energy type within range and can dismiss it by spending 1 AP:

  • Your voice booms up to 3 times louder than normal.

  • You summon harmless magic of your chosen energy type to swirl around you in a visual display

  • Your eyes glow with your chosen energy type.

Spell Enhancements

Multiple Effects: (1 MP) You can have up to all 3 of the effects going at once.

VdMM8G4AJibZFxIT.name: Multiple Effects
VdMM8G4AJibZFxIT.description: You can have up to all 3 of the effects going at once.

system.description:
You speak a command to a creature within range that can hear you.

Make a Spell Check against the target’s Charisma Save.

Save Failure: The target immediately takes a 1 AP Action of your choice for free and loses 1 AP if they have any available. If the Action targets a creature or object, you choose the target

DC Tip: At the GM’s discretion you can have the target perform something specific that isn’t a listed Action. For example, you could have the creature perform 2 Minor Actions.

The creature can’t willingly fail their Check or spend their own resources (AP, SP, or MP) to modify the Action it takes. The Spell has no effect if the target is unable to perform the Action (such as commanding an Immobilized creature to move), or if the Action is directly harmful to itself (such as Attacking itself or moving for the purpose of provoking Opportunity Attacks).

Spell Enhancements

Range: (1 AP, Repeatable) The range of the Spell increases by 5 Spaces. You can use this Enhancement a maximum of two times.

Perfect Command: (1 MP) The Spell no longer fails if the commanded Actions are directly harmful to the target.

Memory Block: (1 MP) The target has no recollection of any Actions taken as a result of this Spell.

Multiple Commands: (2 MP, Repeatable) The target takes up to 1 additional Action of your choice for free. Actions taken as part of this Spell subjected to the Multiple Check Penalty. The target loses 1 additional AP if they are able.

System Tip: MCP is not automated for this.

Extended Command: (2 MP, Repeatable) A taken Action can cost up to 1 additional AP. If used with Multiple Commands you increase the cost of only one taken Action. The target loses 1 additional AP if they are able.

DC Tip: If a chosen Action costs more than expected (such as a Dragon’s Breath Action costing 3 AP when you thought it was 2 AP), you can choose another Action for the creature to take.

Example: You spend 4 MP to use Multiple Commands and Extended Command, on a failed Save you can command the target to take 2 Actions, one from the original Spell and one from Multiple Commands, you can then increase the AP cost of either Action by 1 using Extended Command. For example, you could have the target Move (1 AP) and then Attack with ADV (2 AP) or you could instead have them use a Fireball (2 AP) and then Attack with a weapon at DisADV due to Multiple Check Penalty (1 AP). The target would then lose a total of up to 3 AP if they’re available.

The target takes up to 1 additional Action of your choice for free. Actions taken as part of this Spell subjected to the Multiple Check Penalty.

The target loses 1 additional AP if they are able.

yYTciZs05KUpVOGb.name: Extended Command
yYTciZs05KUpVOGb.description:
A taken Action can cost up to 1 additional AP. If used with Multiple Commands you increase the cost of only one taken Action. The target loses 1 additional AP if they are able.

DC Tip: If a chosen Action costs more than expected (such as a Dragon’s Breath Action costing 3 AP when you thought it was 2 AP), you can choose another Action for the creature to take.

Example: You spend 4 MP to use Multiple Commands and Extended Command, on a failed Save you can command the target to take 2 Actions, one from the original Spell and one from Multiple Commands, you can then increase the AP cost of either Action by 1 using Extended Command. For example, you could have the target Move (1 AP) and then Attack with ADV (2 AP) or you could instead have them use a Fireball (2 AP) and then Attack with a weapon at DisADV due to Multiple Check Penalty (1 AP). The target would then lose a total of up to 3 AP if they’re available.

system.description:
You conjure a bolt of pure magical energy that homes in on a target.

Make a Ranged Spell Attack against the AD of a target within range.

Hit: The target takes 1 Bludgeoning, Slashing or Piercing damage (your choice).

Spell Enhancements

Range: (1 AP, Repeatable) The range of the Spell increases by 5 Spaces. You can use this Enhancement a maximum of two times.

Damage: (1 AP, Repeatable) The damage increases by 1 for 1 target of your choice.

Arcane Missiles: (1 AP + X MP) Choose X additional targets within range using the same Spell Attack for all targets. The AP cost of this Enhancement can’t be paid for with MP (See Mana Points on AP Enhancements).

Autonomous: (1 MP) You can Attack a target you have seen since the start of your last turn, provided it’s within range and there is a path between you and the target the projectile can follow. This attack ignores 1/2 or 3/4 Cover and you don’t have DisADV if the target is Unseen by you.

system.description:
You accelerate the natural healing process of a target within range. Make a DC 15 Spell Check.

Failure: The target can spend up to 1 Rest Point, regaining HP equal to the Rest Points Spent.

Success: The target can spend up to 2 Rest Points.

Success (each 5): The target can spend 1 additional Rest Point.

Spell Enhancements

Range: (1 AP, Repeatable) The range of the Spell increases by 5 Spaces. You can use this Enhancement a maximum of two times.

Increased Healing: (1 AP, Repeatable) The target can spend 2 additional Rest Points.

Repeat Save: (1 AP) The target can make a Repeated Save against an effect they are subjected to.

Uy168TephejSRD6o.name: Range
Uy168TephejSRD6o.description: The range of the Spell increases by 5 Spaces. You can use this Enhancement a maximum of two times.

YFaTpeoYKFpu6NR5.name: Increased Healing
YFaTpeoYKFpu6NR5.description: The target can spend 2 additional Rest Points.

iCSQFrBp4jqcHQrT.name: Repeat Save
iCSQFrBp4jqcHQrT.description: The target can make a Repeated Save against an effect they are subjected to.

system.description:
You produce a torrent of fire that envelops a 3 Space Cone. Make an Area Spell Attack against the AD of every target within the area.

Hit: The target takes 1 Fire damage.

Spell Passive: Ignite

Flammable objects that are not being worn or held in the target’s Space catch fire. A creature can spend 1 AP to put out a mundane fire within 1 Space of them.

Beta Note: For now, you can assume objects remain on fire for 1 minute and spreads to other flammable objects at a rate of 1 Space per minute unless the GM decided otherwise. A creature does not start Burning when it’s in a Space with a burning object unless the entire Space is on Fire.

Spell Enhancements

Damage: (1 MP, Repeatable) The damage increases by 1.

Area: (1 MP, Repeatable) The length of the Cone increases by 1 Space.

Burning: (X MP) Each target makes a Repeated Physical Save. Save Failure: The target begins Burning X for 1 minute.

Back Draft: (1 MP) Each target makes a Might Save against your Save DC. Save Failure: The target is pushed 1 Space away from you. Failure (each 5): The target is pushed 1 additional Space.

xy4D9s4wzlhzuoSB.name: Area
xy4D9s4wzlhzuoSB.description: The length of the Cone increases by 1 Space.

aDZhJGh1dd7JYwNs.name: Burning
aDZhJGh1dd7JYwNs.description:
Each target makes a Repeated Physical Save.

Save Failure: The target begins Burning X for 1 minute.

addsEffect.name: Burning - Fire Torrent
addsEffect.description:
You take X Fire damage at the start of each of your turns.

Ending Burning: All stacks of the Condition end when you're doused by at least 1 gallon (4 liters) of water or fully immersed in water. Alternatively, a creature within 1 Space can spend 1 AP to remove 1 stack of the Condition.

KwCOThJtoX3w2hOm.name: Back Draft
KwCOThJtoX3w2hOm.description:
Each target makes a Might Save against your Save DC.

Save Failure: The target is pushed 1 Space away from you.

Failure (each 5): The target is pushed 1 additional Space.

system.description:
You conjure a bolt of fire that flies towards a target of your choice. Make a Ranged Spell Attack against the PD of a target within range.

Hit: The target takes 1 Fire damage.

Spell Passive: Ignite

Flammable objects that are not being worn or held in the target’s Space catch fire. A creature can spend 1 AP to put out a mundane fire within 1 Space of them.

Beta Note: For now, you can assume objects remain on fire for 1 minute and spreads to other flammable objects at a rate of 1 Space per minute unless the GM decided otherwise. A creature does not start Burning when it’s in a Space with a burning object unless the entire Space is on Fire.

Spell Enhancements

Range: (1 AP, Repeatable) The range of the Spell increases by 5 Spaces. You can use this Enhancement a maximum of two times.

Damage: (1 AP, Repeatable) The damage increases by 1 for 1 target of your choice.

Burning: (1 AP, Repeatable) 1 target of your choice makes a Repeated Physical Save. Save Failure: The target begins Burning for 1 minute.

Flaming Missile: (1 AP + X MP) Choose X additional targets within range using the same Spell Attack for all targets. The AP cost of this Enhancement can’t be paid for with MP (See Mana Points on AP Enhancements).

5wsXZPd9GlzvLhc2.name: Range
5wsXZPd9GlzvLhc2.description: The range of the Spell increases by 5 Spaces. You can use this Enhancement a maximum of two times.

SVWeUvKNMbm21AzG.description: The damage increases by 1 for 1 target of your choice.

fNgCrizDvqy9DFUy.description:
1 target of your choice makes a Repeated Physical Save.

Save Failure: The target begins Burning for 1 minute.

dsjucb4gK7IsbFDB.description: Choose X additional targets within range using the same Spell Attack for all targets. The AP cost of this Enhancement can’t be paid for with MP (See Mana Points on AP Enhancements).

system.description: You can spend 1 AP to make 1 Attack Check.

You can spend 1 AP to make an Attack Check Contested by the target's Athletics, Acrobatics, or Trickery Check (target’s choice).

The target has ADV if they’re holding the object with 2 hands. You have DisADV if the target is larger than you. You can’t Disarm a creature that’s 2 Sizes larger than you.

Success: The targeted object falls into an unoccupied space of your choice within 1 Space of the creature.

rAhzI1qOxxsXLX17.name: Size Check

system.description:
You can spend 1 AP to throw an object or a creature you have Grappled.

Object

The target is thrown up to a number of Spaces away as shown in the Throwing Table. For the purposes of throwing, your minimum Might is 1. The total distance thrown is halved if thrown vertically.

Throw at a Target: When you throw an object at a target it’s considered a Ranged Martial Attack with an Improvised Weapon against the target’s PD.

  • Hit: The target takes 1 damage of a type determined by the GM.

  • Miss: The thrown object continues traveling up to your maximum throwing distance.

DC Tip: When you throw Weapons (including an Improvised Weapon) that lack the Toss or Thrown Properties as part of an Attack, you make the Attack with DisADV.

Creature

Make an Athletics Check against the target’s Might Save.

  • Check Success: The target is thrown up to a number of Spaces away as shown in the Throwing Table (ending the Grapple).

For the purposes of throwing, your minimum Might is 1. The total distance thrown is halved if thrown vertically upward.

Throw at a Target: When you throw the creature at a target, you compare your Athletics Check against the target’s PD.

  • Hit: The thrown creature and the target share the Collision damage (each take half).

  • Miss: The thrown creature continues traveling to your maximum throwing distance.

Throwing Table

Size

Spaces Thrown

2+ Sizes smaller

2 x Might

1 Size smaller

Might

Same Size

1/2 Might

Larger

Impossible

Example: A Medium sized creature with a Might of 0 (minimum Might of 1) could throw a Tiny object up to 2 Spaces, a Small object 1 Space, and Medium object 1 Space (rounded up from 0.5). Conversely, if that creature had a Might of 6, they could throw a Tiny object 12 Spaces, a Small object 6 Spaces, and a Medium object 3 Spaces.

System Info: In the DC20 system, throwing is not a basic action and has its own section in the rules. For automation purposes, I added it to the actions.
You can spend 1 AP to impose DisADV on Opportunity Attacks made against you until the start of your next turn.

effects.name: Disengage
effects.description: You impose DisADV on Opportunity Attacks made against you until the start of your next turn.

system.description:
If you move at least 2 Spaces in a straight line, you can spend 1 AP to attempt to Tackle a creature that’s your size or smaller.

Make an Athletics Check contested by the target's Martial Check.

Success: You Grapple the target and you both move 1 Space in the same direction you were moving then immediately fall Prone.

Success (each 5): +1 Space moved.
You can spend 2 AP to become immune to Opportunity Attacks until the start of your next turn.

effects.name: Full Disengage
effects.description: You are immune to Opportunity Attacks until the start of your next turn.

system.description:
Using a free hand, you can spend 1 AP to attempt to Grapple another creature within 1 Space.

Make an Athletics Check contested by the opposing creature’s Martial Check.

You or the target may have ADV or DisADV on your Check based on each other’s size (see @UUID[Compendium.dc20rpg.rules.JournalEntry.HNPA8Fd7ynirYUBq.JournalEntryPage.ae17b973b3872de1]{Moving & Grappling Creatures}).

  • Success: The creature is Grappled by you.

Moving the Target

You can move the target to any Space adjacent to you by spending your own Movement to do so. Alternatively, when you move, you can move the target with you, but you’re considered Slowed (Every 1 Space you move costs an extra 1 Space of movement).

Multiple Grapplers: If you attempt to move or throw a target (see Throw Creature Maneuver) that’s also Grappled by 1 or more other creatures, you must make a Contested Athletics Check against all the other Grapplers.

  • Success: You move or throw the target as normal and break the Grapple of all other creatures who were also Grappling that target.

Ending a Grapple

Escape Grapple: The target can spend 1 AP to attempt to free itself from the Grapple. When it does, it makes an Acrobatics or Athletics Check contested by your Athletics Check. Success: The Grapple immediately ends.

Incapacitated Grappler: If you become Incapacitated, the Grapple immediately ends.

Forced Movement: If an effect attempts to forcibly move the target beyond your reach, you make the Check or Save instead of the target. If the effect targets both you and the target, you make 1 Check or Save for both of you.

  • Success: The targets of the effect are not moved.

  • Failure: The Grapple immediately ends, and the targets of the effect are moved.

Falling: If the target begins falling (and you don’t), the Grapple ends if you can’t carry the target’s weight. If you can carry its weight, you hold it suspended in the air.

You can spend 1 AP to attempt to Hide from 1 or more creatures that can’t see you (Unseen).

Make a Stealth Check against the opposing creature’(s) Passive Awareness.

Success: You become Hidden from creatures whose Passive Awareness you beat (making you Unseen and Unheard by them).

You remain Hidden until you make a noise louder than a whisper, make an Attack, cast a Spell with a Verbal Component, or a creature takes the Search Action and successfully locates you.

See @UUID[Compendium.dc20rpg.rules.JournalEntry.HNPA8Fd7ynirYUBq.JournalEntryPage.a0d67820b3677829]{Hidden Creatures} (@UUID[Compendium.dc20rpg.rules.JournalEntry.HNPA8Fd7ynirYUBq.JournalEntryPage.6396da38cde5e264]{Unseen}, @UUID[Compendium.dc20rpg.rules.JournalEntry.HNPA8Fd7ynirYUBq.JournalEntryPage.8c1b9e075707a2ce]{Unheard}, @UUID[Compendium.dc20rpg.rules.JournalEntry.HNPA8Fd7ynirYUBq.JournalEntryPage.38850187f9364dd3]{Hidden}) for more on hiding.

When you take the Hide Action and beat an enemy’s Passive Awareness, you only become Hidden from that creature because it can’t see or hear you (you’re both Unseen and Unheard). However, if your Stealth Check beats one enemy’s Awareness Score but not another’s, then you’re Hidden from the first enemy, but you’re not Hidden from the second enemy.

effects.name: Hidden
effects.description: You become Hidden from creatures whose Passive Awareness you beat.

system.description:
You can spend 1 AP to move up to your Speed in Spaces (default of 5).

You choose where to move, and can break up your movement by moving before and after taking a different Action.

You can’t end your turn in a Space occupied by another creature.

A creature with 4 AP can take the Move Action to move 1 Space, take the Attack Action, move 1 Space again (2 total Spaces), take the Attack Action again, and then move 2 Spaces again (4 total Spaces).

TXdeNOrH8C8yuOHh.name: preItemRoll

system.description:
You can spend 1 AP to grant another creature a d8 Help Die that lasts until the start of your next turn. Upon granting the Help Die, you must declare which creature you’re Helping and the type of Check you’ll be aiding them with while meeting the following conditions:

  • Attack: You declare 1 target for the Attack. You must be within 1 Space of the Attacker or the target of the Attack. While the Help Die lasts, it can be added to an Attack made against the target.

  • Skill or Trade Check: You declare a type of Skill or Trade Check. You describe how you’re Helping them and must do so with a Skill or Trade that you have at least 1 Mastery Level in. You can use the same Skill or Trade or a different one.

The Help Die can only be used to aid the type of Check declared and disappears once it is used.

Multiple Help Penalty: Once you take the Help Action, each time you take the Help Action again before the end of your turn, your Help Die decays by 1 step, to a minimum of a d4 (d8 | d6 | d4). These Help Dice only decay when using the Help Action. Help Die granted by other sources (such as the Sword Maneuver) decay independently of any Help Dice grant through the Help Action.
You can spend 1 AP to impose DisADV on the next Attack or Grapple made against you before the start of your next turn.

effects.name: Dodge
effects.description: You impose DisADV on the next Attack or Grapple made against you before the start of your next turn.

system.description: You can spend 2 AP to impose DisADV on all Attacks or Grapples made against you until the start of your next turn.

effects.name: Full Dodge
effects.description: You impose DisADV on all Attacks or Grapples made against you until the start of your next turn.

system.description: You can spend 1 or more AP to cast a Spell that you know. If the Spell has a Mana Point requirement, you must spend that much MP to cast the Spell.

You can spend 1 AP to attempt to push a creature within 1 Space of you. Make an Athletics Check contested by the target’s Martial Check.

Success: You push the creature 1 Space away from you or to its left or right.

Success (each 5): Push up to 1 additional Space.

Knock Prone: After the result, you can choose to reduce the total distance the target is pushed by 1 Space to knock them Prone instead.

DC Tip: If you shove a creature into a target, follow the rules for hitting the target in the Collision section.

You can spend 1 AP to attempt to recall or discern some information about a creature that you can see or hear. Make a DC 10 Knowledge Check.

Success: You learn a piece of lore about the creature.

Success (each 5): You learn 1 creature statistic (PD, AD, Attacks, Abilities, Resistances, Vulnerabilities, Immunities, etc.).

You can spend 1 AP to attempt to beguile a Beast that can see or hear you. Make an Animal Check contested by the target’s Charisma Save.

Success: The animal is Taunted by you for 1 minute (Repeated Save) or until you target it with a harmful Attack, Spell, or other effect.

Success (5): It’s also Impaired.

Success (10): It’s also Charmed.

effects.description: You have DisADV on Attack Checks against creatures other than the one that Taunted you. If a creature is successfully Taunted while already Taunted by another creature, the original Taunt is removed.

system.description:
You can spend 1 AP to make a Trickery Check Contested by the target’s Insight Check.

Success: The next Attack against the target before the start of your next turn has ADV and deals +1 damage.

effects.name: Feint
effects.description: The next Attack against you has ADV and deals +1 damage.

system.description:
You can spend 1 AP to attempt to discern the course of actions a creature might take on its next turn.

Make an Insight Check contested by the target’s Trickery or Influence Check (its choice).

Success: You learn the target’s emotional state and whether it plans to make an Attack, cast a Spell, or flee combat during its next turn.

Success (5): You know who the creature is likely to target with a harmful ability.

Success (10): You know which ability the creature plans to use.

If the target is a PC, its player tells you what the creature plans to do. If the target is an NPC, the GM tells you what the creature plans to do.

You can spend 1 AP to attempt to uncover a concealed object on a creature, a secret compartment, or the intended function of a mechanism within 1 Space of you.

  • Concealed Objects: You can attempt to uncover any objects concealed on a creature.

    Make an Investigation Check contested by the target’s Trickery Check.

    Success: You know the location of any concealed object on the creature.

  • Secret Compartments: You can attempt to uncover any secret compartments. Make an Investigation Check against the discovery DC of any secret compartments.

    Success: You discover the location of any secret compartments whose discovery DC you beat.

  • Discern Mechanism: You can attempt to discern the functionality of a mechanism (the effect of a trap, how to open a secret door, or activate a device). Make an Investigation Check.

    Success: You learn how the mechanism works and the methods to activate and disable it (if any).

You can spend 1 AP to perform 1 of the following object interactions:

  • Drink a Potion or administer a Potion to another creature.

  • Attempt to lock or unlock a Lock.

  • Make a Trickery Check to activate or disable a trap or other mechanism.

  • Transfer an item to or from another creature (only 1 of the two creatures spends 1 AP).

You can spend 1 AP to touch a creature and tend to its wounds.

Make a DC 10 Medicine Check.

Success: You stop its Bleeding or Stabilize it (your choice).

Success (each 5): The creature gains +1 Temp HP.

You can spend 1 AP to attempt to hide an object on yourself or in nearby foliage, debris, or decor to render it Hidden.

Make a contested Trickery Check against the Passive Awareness of creatures that can see you.

Success: The object is Hidden from any creature whose Passive Awareness you beat.

You can spend 1 AP to attempt to intimidate a creature that can see or hear you within 10 Spaces.

Make an Intimidation Check contested by the target’s Charisma Save.

Success: The target is Intimidated by you until the end of your next turn.

When you perform a Jump, you can spend 1 AP to increase your Jump Distance for the Jump.

Make a DC 10 Acrobatics Check.

  • Failure: Your Jump Distance increases by 1.

  • Success: Your Jump Distance increases by 2.

  • Success (each 5): +1 Jump Distance.

DC Tip: Remember, your Long Jump Distance equals your Jump Distance in Spaces, and your High Jump distance equals your Jump Distance in feet.

You can spend 1 AP to attempt to taunt a creature that can see or hear you within 10 Spaces.

Make an Influence Check contested by the target’s Charisma Save.

Success: The target is Taunted by you for 1 Round.

Prerequisite: Martial Path

Trigger: A creature you can see within your Melee Range uses its movement to leave your Melee Range, draws a Weapon or Spell Focus, picks an item up off the ground, or takes the Object Action.

Reaction: You can spend 1 AP to make a Melee Martial Attack against the provoking creature.

DC Tip: You can spend additional AP to gain ADV or to perform Maneuvers with the Attack.

Prerequisite: Spellcasting Path

During a Spell Duel, the creature casting the Spell is referred to as the Caster and the opposing creature is referred to as the Challenger.

If multiple creatures attempt to initiate a Spell Duel, one of them must be chosen as the Challenger, while the others assist them through Combo Casting. Additional creatures can also choose to assist the Caster when a Spell Duel is initiated (see Combo Casting for more information).

Trigger: You see an MP Effect being cast, provided the Caster or a target is within 5 Spaces of you.

Reaction: You can spend an amount of AP and 1 or more MP (up to your Mana Spend Limit) to attempt to stop the Caster’s MP Effect. The amount of AP spent is equal to the base AP cost of the MP Effect you are trying to stop.

Contest

Make a Spell Check contested by the Spell Check or Spell Attack that the Caster makes to cast their MP Effect.

If the Caster’s MP Effect doesn’t require a Check, they have to make a Spell Check as a Reaction as part of the Spell Duel.

For the purposes of the Spell Duel, the Caster and Challenger each gain a bonus to their Check equal to twice the MP spent they spent respectively.

Beta Note: Since enemies don’t have MP costs for their MP Effects you can assume that their MP Effects cost an amount of MP equal to half their Level (rounded up).

Outcomes

Success: The Caster’s MP Effect fails and has no effect.

Failure: The Caster’s MP Effect succeeds and takes effect. The Check bonus gained from MP spent doesn’t apply to the MP Effect’s Success and Failure Statements.

Tie: The Caster’s MP Effect fails, has no effect, and you each roll on the @UUID[Compendium.dc20rpg.roll-tables.RollTable.HxkcpDT6pEOIfYUk]{Wild Magic Surge Table}. The effect from the table lasts for 1 Round.

Resources Lost: Whatever the result, each creature still spends all AP, MP, or other resources as normal.

Example: A Caster makes an MP based Spell Attack and a Challenger attempts to stop it. The Caster’s Spell Attack cost 1 AP and 2 MP. The Challenger must spend 1 AP and at least 1 MP to attempt to stop the Spell Attack. The Caster makes an Attack Check (since its a Spell Attack) and the Challenger makes a Spell Check. The Caster gets a +4 bonus to their Attack Check (2 MP spent) and the Challenger gets a +2 bonus to their Spell Check (1 MP spent). Should the Caster win the Contest, their MP Effect takes effect but they don’t include the +4 bonus from MP spent to determine if they score a Heavy Hit or higher.

Yalv653kxQPs5FZf.name: Trigger Spell Duel

system.description:
You must spend 1 AP at the start of each of your turns to Sustain an effect until the start of your next turn.

You can't Sustain an effect while Dazed. When you become Dazed, any effect you’re Sustaining ends. You can choose to end the effect at anytime for free.

Outside of Combat: You can only Sustain 1 effect at a time outside of Combat. If you attempt to Sustain a second effect while already Sustaining a first, the first effect immediately ends.
You can spend 1 MP to add 2 AP worth of AP Enhancements to a Spell. You can do this multiple times per Spell.

vprOl4a2bVE14Qps.name: Spend MP on AP Enhancements
vprOl4a2bVE14Qps.description: You can spend 1 MP to add 2 AP worth of AP Enhancements to a Spell. You can do this multiple times per Spell.

system.description:
You can spend 1 AP to maneuver a mount you’re riding to avoid danger.

Make a DC 10 Animal Check.

Success: The mount’s PD increases by 2 until the start of your next turn.

Success (5): +2 PD.

Success (10): +4 PD.

effects.name: Mounted Defense
effects.description: The mount’s PD increases by 2 until the start of your next turn.

effects.name: Mounted Defense +2
effects.description: The mount’s PD increases by 4 until the start of your next turn.

effects.name: Mounted Defense +4
effects.description: The mount’s PD increases by 6 until the start of your next turn.

system.description:
You can spend 1 AP to attempt to move through a Space occupied by a hostile creature that’s within 1 size of you.

Make a contested Martial Check against the target.

Success: You can move through the creature’s Space as if it were Difficult Terrain (Slowed while moving through the area).

Success (5): You can move through the creature's space without it counting as Difficult Terrain.

You can spend 1 AP to attempt to locate 1 or more Hidden creatures and concealed objects within your Line of Sight.

  • Hidden Creatures: You attempt to locate any Hidden creatures in the area. Make an Awareness Check against the Stealth Check of any Hidden creatures.

    Success: You know the location of any Hidden creature whose Stealth Check you beat until the end of your turn. Knowing a creature’s location does not grant you the ability to see it.

  • Hidden Objects: You attempt to locate any Hidden objects in the area. Make an Awareness Check against the DC to discover any concealed objects (such as traps, secret doors, or hidden items).

    Success: You discover the location of any Hidden object whose discovery DC you beat.

Without obstruction, a PC’s line of sight could reach the horizon. You might ask your PC to make their Awareness Check with DisADV to detect a creature that’s far away, such as beyond 10 Spaces.

All characters with Weapon Combat Training gain access to the following Martial Enhancements:

  • Damage: The Attack deals +1 damage against 1 target of the Attack.

  • Range: The Attack’s Range increase by 1 Space if it’s a Melee Attack, or by 5 Spaces if it’s a Ranged Attack. This Enhancement can only be performed once per Attack.

  • Daze: A creature of your choice targeted by the Attack makes a Might Save against your Save DC. Save Failure: The target becomes Dazed until the end of their next turn.

DC Tip: Remember, a Dazed creature can’t Sustain any effects at the start of their turn! While Martial characters can’t stop a Spell from being cast like a Spellcaster can with a Spell Duel, a Martial Character can more easily disrupt a Sustained Spell by causing the Spellcaster to lose focus.

Example: You spend 2 AP and 1 SP on an Attack against an enemy creature. 1 AP to take the Attack Action (make 1 Attack Check), 1 AP to add the Martial Enhancement: Damage to deal +1 damage, and 1 SP to add the Martial Enhancement: Range to grant your Attack some extra range.
